intro: Ixora arborescens, also known as the flame of the woods, is a flowering plant species in the Rubiaceae family, native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Ixora arborescens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ixora arborescens is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat various health conditions.
